hypothesis: If a diffusion model framework combining an equivariant graph neural network backbone with an energy-based denoising approach and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement is applied to amorphous material generation, then it will produce structurally valid amorphous configurations that accurately reproduce experimentally observed radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, and structure factors, because the energy-based score estimation with HMC equilibration enforces physically consistent local geometry during the reverse diffusion process.
